title: EU MiFID Suitability Assessment Framework
content:
This framework establishes Meridian Wealth Partners' standardized approach for conducting client suitability assessments for moderate-risk equity investments in compliance with MiFID II Article 25(2) and applicable ESMA Guidelines. All associate-level advisors and above must adhere to these procedures when recommending equity products classified as moderate risk.

Client Knowledge and Experience Assessment: Associates must evaluate clients' understanding of equity markets, including familiarity with dividend yields, price-to-earnings ratios, and market volatility. Clients demonstrating less than two years of equity investment experience or inability to explain basic concepts such as market capitalization or sector diversification require enhanced disclosure and documentation. The assessment must cover previous transactions in similar instruments, education level, and professional background relevant to financial markets.

Financial Situation Evaluation: Documentation must include verified annual income, liquid net worth excluding primary residence, and existing investment portfolio composition. For moderate-risk equity recommendations, clients should maintain liquid assets representing at least six months of expenses outside their investment portfolio. The firm requires minimum investable assets of €50,000 for individual equity positions and €25,000 for diversified equity funds. Associates must verify income sources and assess stability, particularly for clients approaching retirement or with irregular earnings.

Investment Objectives and Risk Tolerance: The suitability assessment must align equity recommendations with stated investment goals, time horizon, and risk capacity. Moderate-risk equity investments are suitable for clients with investment horizons exceeding three years and tolerance for potential losses of 15-25% over 12-month periods. Associates must document specific objectives such as capital growth, income generation, or portfolio diversification. Risk tolerance questionnaires must be completed within 12 months and updated following significant life events or market volatility exceeding 20%.

Product-Specific Considerations: When recommending individual equities, associates must ensure clients understand concentration risk and market volatility. Single stock positions should not exceed 5% of total portfolio value for moderate-risk classifications. Diversified equity funds and ETFs may comprise up to 40% of suitable client portfolios. Geographic diversification requirements mandate no more than 60% exposure to any single market or currency for moderate-risk equity allocations.

Documentation Requirements: All suitability assessments must include completed risk profiling questionnaire, verified financial statements or tax returns from the most recent year, and documented rationale linking client circumstances to specific equity recommendations. Associates must record any client limitations or preferences, such as ESG criteria or sector exclusions, and explain how recommendations accommodate these requirements. Regular portfolio reviews are mandatory every 18 months or following material changes in client circumstances.

Ongoing Monitoring: Associates must establish systematic review processes to ensure continued suitability of equity holdings. Quarterly performance reviews should assess whether holdings remain aligned with client objectives and risk tolerance. Significant portfolio deviation from target allocation or individual position gains exceeding 8% of total portfolio value trigger mandatory rebalancing discussions. Material changes in issuer fundamentals, including earnings revisions beyond ±15% or credit rating changes, require immediate client notification and suitability reassessment.
